'''Taylor Kerr''' (born 1 August 2006) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/team-squad-wigan-0-desc.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

He has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/a-youngster-to-watch-out-for-at-every-2026-super-league-club-including-wigan-warriors-leeds-rhinos-and-hull-fc-hopefuls-110976/ A youngster to watch out for at every 2026 Super League club including Wigan Warriors, Leeds Rhinos and Hull FC hopefuls]</ref>

==Background==
Kerr was born in [[Wigan, Greater Manchester]], England. His cousin [[Brad Dwyer]] is a fellow professional rugby league footballer.<ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/salford-red-devils-loan-duo-1325308 Salford Red Devils loan duo answer SOS call and share special family moment]</ref>

He played for [[Ince Rose Bridge]], [[Orrell St Jame]]s and [[Wigan St Judes]] as a junior.<ref>{{cite news | url = https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/meet-the-wigan-warriors-academy-product-who-has-already-played-at-wembley-5047505 | title = Meet the Wigan Warriors academy product who has already played at Wembley | newspaper = Wigan Today | date = 24 March 2025 | first = Drew | last = Darbyshire }}</ref> He played for [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in the [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Origin series]].<ref>[https://www.salfordreddevils.com/makin-and-kerr-join-on-loan-from-wigan/ Makin and Kerr join on loan from Wigan]</ref>

==Career==
Kerr scored five tries in 13 appearances for the Wigan Warriors Academy side in 2023 and later joined the first team squad ahead of the 2025 season.<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nextgen-meet-the-wigan-warriors-prodigy-being-mentored-by-sean-oloughlin NextGen: Meet the Wigan Warriors prodigy being mentored by Sean O'Loughlin]</ref> 

He made his professional debut in February 2025 on loan at the [[London Broncos]] against [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[2025 RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].<ref>[https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2025/03/14/kerr-previews-reserves-derby/ Kerr previews Reserves derby]</ref>

In August 2025 Kerr was loaned out by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] to the [[Salford Red Devils]], during which time he made his [[Super League]] debut for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] against [[Hull FC]].<ref>[https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-duo-among-six-super-league-youngsters-loaned-to-struggling-salford-red-devils-5264126 Wigan Warriors duo among six Super League youngsters loaned to struggling Salford Red Devils]</ref>
